plan.visual_reading: The arc opens on the wrenched, stretched body on the cross — the thing the psalm describes — then the aged king who wrote it sits seeing a death not his own. The quoted lines land on the drained 'poured out' body and the countable ribs; the cold onlookers stare up; one strained shoulder shows a body hung by its arms. The NT-anchored echoes deepen it: the pierced side (Zech 12:10 -> John 19:37) and the striped back (Isa 53:5 -> 1 Pet 2:24). A diptych holds king and cross a thousand years apart, the nailed hand bears the marks, and the hero — the full crucified Christ — is the one crushed in our place. It lands on the freedom that buys, the open road home, and the cross at dawn.
plan.red_team_notes: Revision: removed the banned token 'frame' from scene 5 (now 'a starved and stretched body'). Body close-ups kept reverent and state-only with traditional loincloth; varied by region and broken up by the king, diptych, onlookers, freed figure and road; the descriptive match carried honestly by the king-seeing-a-death and the diptych.

scenes.title: Out of Joint
scenes.purpose: open on the wrenched, stretched body the psalm describes
scenes.rationale: the most arresting topic image — the dislocated stretch of a crucified body
scenes.visible_elements: stretched taut arms, shoulders pulled from their sockets, drawn-long torso, fallen head
scenes.emotional_tone: a body wrenched out of joint
scenes.subject_block: a crucified man seen from a low side angle, the arms stretched taut and straight along the dark beam, the shoulders pulled hard from their sockets by the hanging weight of the body, the torso drawn long, a thin torn loincloth at the hips, the head fallen to one side, deep shadow behind, the strain of every joint held in stillness
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a body wrenched out of joint

scenes.title: The King Who Wrote It
scenes.purpose: David seeing a death not his own as he writes the psalm
scenes.rationale: the 'a king described it' hook made visible, not a generic scribe
scenes.visible_elements: an aged king over an open scroll by lamplight, a harp, a haunted faraway look
scenes.emotional_tone: a king seeing a death not his own
scenes.subject_block: an aged king in a worn royal robe seated low over an open scroll by lamplight, a feather pen at rest in his hand, his face lifted with a haunted faraway look as if seeing a death not his own, a small harp set at his side, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting a distant cross on a far hill, a crown set aside, a stretched dying figure, and a far gathering of onlookers, the king dominant and the vision half-dissolved behind him
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a king seeing a death not his own
macro_elements: the small harp at his side
vignettes: a distant cross on a far hill

scenes.title: Poured Out Like Water
scenes.rationale: the quoted line rests on the poured-out body
scenes.visible_elements: fallen head, sweat-sheened drained chest, a thin trail down the side
scenes.emotional_tone: a body poured out like water
scenes.subject_block: the crucified man's head and chest seen close, the head fallen to one side, the skin sheened with sweat and water, the body drained and slack, a thin trail of water and blood down the side, the gaunt chest still, deep shadow around, the utter spentness of a body poured out
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a body poured out like water
macro_elements: the thin trail down the side

scenes.title: Crushed in Your Place
scenes.purpose: the gospel pivot — the full crucified Christ, the one crushed in our place
scenes.rationale: the CTA's destination; iconic, reverent, bookends the cut
scenes.visible_elements: the full crucified Christ from a low angle, head bowed, arms taut on the beam, a faint light behind the head
scenes.emotional_tone: the one crushed in our place
scenes.subject_block: the full crucified Christ from a low angle against a dark sky, the body stretched on the cross with the head bowed, stripped to a thin loincloth, the arms taut along the beam, a faint light behind the lowered head, the bare hill at the base, an iconic and reverent tableau of the one crushed in our place
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of the one crushed in our place
macro_elements: the stretched arms on the beam
the faint light behind the head

scenes.title: I May Tell All My Bones
scenes.purpose: the stretched ribcage, every bone countable (v17a)
scenes.rationale: the quoted line on the gaunt, stretched torso
scenes.visible_elements: countable ribs, drawn-tight skin, stretched flank, shadowed hollows between bones
scenes.emotional_tone: a body whose every bone can be counted
scenes.subject_block: the crucified man's torso seen close, the ribcage drawn tight beneath thin skin so that every rib stands countable, the body stretched long by the hanging arms, the flesh gaunt and shadowed between the bones, a thin torn loincloth at the hips, the stark visibility of a starved and stretched body
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a body whose every bone can be counted

scenes.title: They Look and Stare
scenes.rationale: the specific staring crowd, not a generic gathering
scenes.visible_elements: hard upward stares, a mocking lip, dangling pierced feet above them
scenes.emotional_tone: cold onlookers staring at a dying man
scenes.subject_block: a cluster of onlookers standing below and gazing up with cold fixed stares, their faces hard and unmoved, one with a faint mocking curl to the lip, the lower edge of a cross and dangling pierced feet just visible above them, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting pointing hands, a turned indifferent back, a soldier's helmet, and a distant temple, the staring faces dominant and the rest half-dissolved
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of cold onlookers staring at a dying man

scenes.title: Hung by the Arms
scenes.purpose: a body hung and pulled by its own weight
scenes.rationale: the mechanism of the dislocation, a distinct macro region
scenes.visible_elements: one rigid stretched arm, a distended shoulder joint, the nailed hand at the beam
scenes.emotional_tone: a body hung and pulled by its own weight
scenes.subject_block: one arm and shoulder of the crucified man seen close, the arm stretched rigid and straight to the nailed hand at the beam, the shoulder joint pulled and distended by the dead weight of the hanging body, the tendons drawn, the skin taut over strained muscle, deep shadow beyond
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a body hung and pulled by its own weight
macro_elements: the nailed hand at the beam

scenes.title: Whom They Pierced
scenes.purpose: looking upon the one they pierced (Zech 12:10 -> John 19:37)
scenes.rationale: a real NT-anchored echo tying the staring to the piercing
scenes.visible_elements: the pierced side with a thin flow, grieving upward-looking faces, a lowered spear
scenes.emotional_tone: looking upon the one they pierced
scenes.subject_block: the pierced side of the crucified man seen near, a dark wound below the ribs with a thin flow, a small group below lifting their faces to look upon him, one woman's face stricken with grief, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting a mourning mother, a soldier's lowered spear, the looking crowd, and a distant temple, the pierced side and the upward-looking faces dominant
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of looking upon the one they pierced
macro_elements: the thin flow from the wound

scenes.title: A Thousand Years Apart
scenes.purpose: hold the king and the cross a thousand years apart, joined by light
scenes.rationale: states 'written early' as composition, not a verbal claim
scenes.visible_elements: the king over his scroll at left, a distant crucified figure at right, a band of light between
scenes.emotional_tone: one death written a thousand years early
scenes.subject_block: a wide composition divided by a vertical band of pale light, at the left the aged king low over his scroll by lamplight, at the right across the divide the distant crucified figure on a dark hill, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting a passing of long years, far generations, a setting sun, and an open scroll, the two halves joined only by the band of light between them
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of one death written a thousand years early

scenes.title: Wounded for Us
scenes.purpose: the stripes borne for us (Isa 53:5 -> 1 Pet 2:24)
scenes.rationale: a real NT-anchored echo for the landing exchange
scenes.visible_elements: a scourged striped back, lowered head, a torn garment fallen from the shoulders
scenes.subject_block: the bowed back and shoulders of the scourged man seen from behind, the skin marked with the dark stripes of a flogging, the head lowered, a torn garment fallen from the shoulders, warm low light across the wounded back against deep shadow, the bearing of stripes borne for others
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of stripes borne for us
macro_elements: the dark stripes across the back

scenes.title: The Marks of One
scenes.purpose: the marks of a cross — the nailed hand
scenes.rationale: a single macro on the nail-wound, the literal mark
scenes.visible_elements: a nailed hand against the beam, iron nail through the palm, dried dark blood
scenes.subject_block: a single nailed hand seen close against the dark wood of the beam, the iron nail driven through the palm, the fingers half-curled, dark blood dried around the wound, the grain of the rough timber beneath, deep shadow around the still hand
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of the marks of a cross
macro_elements: the iron nail through the palm

scenes.title: Crushed So Another Goes Free
scenes.purpose: the exchange — one crushed so another goes free
scenes.rationale: the gospel of the landing made visible
scenes.visible_elements: the central cross, a freed figure rising with loosened hands, broken chains
scenes.emotional_tone: one crushed so another goes free
scenes.subject_block: the cross at the centre with the crucified figure calm in completion, and below it a freed figure rising with loosened hands, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting broken chains, an open door with light beyond, a kneeling man set free, and an open scroll, the central cross dominant and the freedom it buys half-dissolved around it
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of one crushed so another goes free
macro_elements: the breaking light at the cross's foot
vignettes: an open door with light beyond

scenes.title: To Bring You Home
scenes.rationale: an open-home image rather than a literal altar call
scenes.visible_elements: a worn open road to a humble lit doorway at dusk, an olive tree
scenes.subject_block: a worn open road leading toward a humble lit doorway at dusk, warm lamplight spilling across the threshold, a single olive tree beside the door, the path clear and inviting, no figure upon it yet, the way home left open and lit
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a way home left open
macro_elements: the warm light on the threshold

scenes.title: Come to Him
scenes.purpose: close on the cross at dawn
scenes.rationale: lands the cut on the finished suffering at daybreak
scenes.visible_elements: the cross against a widening dawn over a quiet land
scenes.subject_block: the cross standing against a widening dawn, the figure calm and still in completion, warm gold light breaking from behind the upright beam across a quiet land, the hill's crest in soft shadow, the single cross high at the centre as the day breaks
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a finished suffering and a breaking dawn

plan.rationale: Same 14-scene set, with the banned token 'frame' removed from scene 5 ('a starved and stretched body'). Nine singles anchor the body beats and five unified scenes carry the wider picture and echoes; two nt-gospel-link (hero crucified, the exchange) and two NT-anchored ot-echo (Zech 12:10->John 19:37; Isa 53:5->1 Pet 2:24) satisfy the mix. Body scenes use the traditional loincloth and stay reverent.

panel.note: Independent read: the body-foretold arc is carried, not just illustrated — wrenched body open, quoted-line proofs, pierced/written-early conviction, crushed-so-free landing.

Echoes are genuinely NT-anchored (John 19:37 quotes Zech 12:10; 1 Pet 2:24 echoes Isa 53:5); descriptive-match guarded by king-seeing-a-death and the diptych. No forced verbal prophecy, no fear/works.
Heavy on crucifixion-body close-ups; reverent and state-only with loincloth, but render with restraint so it reads as devotional, not clinical or lurid.
jesus_variant 'passion' consistent across crucified scenes; king and road correctly null.
Banned token 'frame' now removed from scene 5; state-only prose, 14 scenes, framings well distributed.
The body focus could turn morbid; the king-who-wrote-it and the freed-figure exchange give it gospel direction beyond suffering.

gates.evidence: Ps 22:14,17 / Zech 12:10 / Isa 53:5 defensible; loincloth traditional; no invented detail.
Every beat id in ['hook', 'point', 'proof', 'conviction', 'landing'] has >=1 supporting scene.
9 single + 5 unified; body regions varied plus king, diptych, onlookers, freed figure, road.
Descriptive match honest; NT-anchored echoes; grace landing.
All subject_block + mood_block content clear of the banned-token list.
